title: Cloning the Self for Mental Well-Being: A Framework for Designing Safe and Therapeutic Self-Clone Chatbots
one_sentence_summary: The paper introduces a design framework for therapeutic self-clone chatbots based on expert and user interviews to enable safe intra-personal exploration for mental well-being.
research_problem: While self-clone chatbots offer novel opportunities for self-derived therapeutic support, they present significant risks including identity confusion, negative reinforcement, blurred user agency, and a lack of established safety guardrails.
method_summary: The researchers conducted in-depth interviews with 16 mental health professionals (providers and researchers) and 6 prospective end-users to explore the affordances, risks, and ethical implications of self-cloning technology.
dataset_or_materials: Qualitative interview data from 16 mental health experts and 6 general users, focusing on individuals with mild, non-clinical mental health symptoms.
main_findings: The study produced a design framework organized around three priorities: grounding the technology in therapeutic models, defining specific design dimensions for self-clones, and establishing safety guardrails to guide responsible development.

section_map.heading: Cloning the Self for Mental Well-Being: A Framework for Designing Safe and Therapeutic Self-Clone Chatbots
